title: Term
short: A single piece of an expression separated by + or −, such as 3x² or −5x.
full:
A term is one of the additive pieces of an expression: in 3x² − 5x + 2 the terms are 3x², −5x and 2. Each term is a coefficient times some power of the variable (the constant 2 is the coefficient of x⁰).

'Like terms' share the same variable power and can be combined — this is the core of simplifying. For example 2x + 3x = 5x.

Terms have no units of their own; they are pure algebra.
